narrative: Dear [Student's Name], as a white male applicant with a middle-income family background and plans to attend law school full-time, we understand how disappointing it must be to receive this rejection notice for our program. The admissions model we use predicts your likelihood of passing the bar exam on the first try, and unfortunately, it estimated a 98% chance that you would fail, which is the key reason your application was denied—our goal is to select students with the strongest predicted odds of bar success. This prediction was driven primarily by your academic performance in school, particularly your low ranking in Year 3 grades compared to your peers, which stood out as the biggest factor pushing the model toward expecting failure. Your LSAT score, which was well below the typical applicant's level, was the second most influential element, further reinforcing the model's concerns about your readiness for the bar. Even your Year 1 grade decile, also in the bottom range relative to averages, added to this risk signal, making it the third strongest contributor toward the failure prediction. In contrast, your undergraduate GPA, though not stellar, actually nudged the prediction slightly in a more positive direction compared to what the model expected. Your middle family income and full-time enrollment plans had minimal sway but leaned slightly helpful. Overall, compared to typical students who get approved—those with mid-to-high school deciles around the 5th or 6th, LSAT scores near 37, and stronger GPAs—your profile in these core academic areas fell notably lower, tipping the scales heavily against you. We know this doesn't capture your full potential or personal story, and it's tough to hear, especially as someone pursuing this path full-time. To move forward, we recommend retaking the LSAT to boost your score significantly, strengthening your undergraduate record if possible through additional coursework, and focusing on improving your academic decile in any ongoing studies—these are the levers that could most dramatically shift future predictions. You're clearly determined, and with targeted improvements in these areas, stronger opportunities await. Keep pushing; many have turned similar profiles around. Best wishes for your next steps.
